article.title: Sun Pharma Strikes $11.75 Billion All-Cash Deal for Organon, the Largest Overseas Acquisition Ever by an Indian Drugmaker
article.summary: India's Sun Pharma will buy Merck spinoff Organon for $14 a share, vaulting into the global top 25 pharma companies and entering biosimilars and women's health at scale.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Sun Pharmaceutical Industries, India's largest drugmaker, has agreed to acquire New Jersey-based Organon & Co. in an all-cash transaction valued at $11.75 billion, according to the [joint announcement issued through BusinessWire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260426881370/en/Sun-Pharma-signs-Definitive-Agreement-to-Acquire-Organon) on April 26, 2026. Under the definitive merger agreement, Organon stockholders will receive $14.00 per share in cash, [as reported by CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/indian-drugmaker-sun-pharma-to-buy-us-firm-organon-in-11point75-billion-deal.html).

The transaction is the biggest biopharma deal announced so far in 2026 and, [according to Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/sun-pharma-to-acquire-us-listed-organon-in-12-billion-deal), the largest overseas acquisition ever made by an Indian pharmaceutical company. Closing is expected in early 2027, subject to Organon stockholder approval and regulatory clearances.

## What We Know

- The enterprise value of the transaction is $11.75 billion, with shareholders receiving $14.00 per share in cash, [per BusinessWire's release of the definitive agreement](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260426881370/en/Sun-Pharma-signs-Definitive-Agreement-to-Acquire-Organon).
- Organon, spun out of Merck in 2021, generated $6.2 billion in 2025 revenue and $1.9 billion in adjusted EBITDA, while carrying $8.6 billion in debt against $574 million in cash, figures [confirmed by Fierce Pharma](https://www.fiercepharma.com/pharma/sun-pharma-strikes-biopharmas-largest-deal-26-1175b-buyout-organon).
- The combined company will have annual revenue of about $12.4 billion, ranking among the world's top 25 pharmaceutical companies, [Bloomberg reports](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/sun-pharma-to-acquire-us-listed-organon-in-12-billion-deal).
- Organon brings more than 70 marketed products distributed in roughly 140 countries, anchored by women's health franchises including the Nexplanon contraceptive implant and the Follistim fertility line, [according to the Organon press release](https://www.organon.com/news/sun-pharma-signs-definitive-agreement-to-acquire-organon/).
- The deal will make Sun Pharma the seventh-largest biosimilars player globally and a top-three competitor in women's health, [per Fierce Pharma's analysis](https://www.fiercepharma.com/pharma/sun-pharma-strikes-biopharmas-largest-deal-26-1175b-buyout-organon).
- Sun Pharma plans to fund the purchase with available cash and committed bank financing, with JPMorgan, Citigroup and MUFG Bank named as financing sources in the [definitive agreement announcement](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260426881370/en/Sun-Pharma-signs-Definitive-Agreement-to-Acquire-Organon).
- Sun Pharma's Mumbai-listed shares rallied on the news, climbing roughly 7 percent in early trading, [CNBC reported](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/indian-drugmaker-sun-pharma-to-buy-us-firm-organon-in-11point75-billion-deal.html).

## Strategic Rationale

For Sun Pharma, the acquisition is a structural pivot away from the U.S. generics business that has long defined the company. Executive Chairman Dilip Shanghvi said in the announcement that "Organon's portfolio, capabilities and global reach are highly complementary to our own," [according to the Organon press release](https://www.organon.com/news/sun-pharma-signs-definitive-agreement-to-acquire-organon/). Managing Director Kirti Ganorkar described the move as a "logical next step in strengthening Sun Pharma's global business" in the same statement.

The deal also expands Sun Pharma's geographic footprint and lifts the combined company into the global top 25 pharmaceutical companies by revenue, [Bloomberg reported](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/sun-pharma-to-acquire-us-listed-organon-in-12-billion-deal). Organon's $6.2 billion in 2025 revenue, generated across 140 countries, reflects a portfolio that spans women's health, biosimilars and established branded medicines, [according to Fierce Pharma](https://www.fiercepharma.com/pharma/sun-pharma-strikes-biopharmas-largest-deal-26-1175b-buyout-organon).

The biosimilars angle is equally significant. Organon's existing oncology biosimilars portfolio gives Sun Pharma immediate scale in a category that has been the subject of a 2026 wave of consolidation, [Fierce Pharma observed](https://www.fiercepharma.com/pharma/sun-pharma-strikes-biopharmas-largest-deal-26-1175b-buyout-organon). The combined company will become the seventh-largest biosimilars seller globally on closing.

## Financial Structure and Risks

The transaction will leave the combined company with meaningful leverage. Organon entered the deal carrying $8.6 billion of debt against $574 million of cash, [Fierce Pharma reported](https://www.fiercepharma.com/pharma/sun-pharma-strikes-biopharmas-largest-deal-26-1175b-buyout-organon). Sun Pharma will fund the purchase using available cash plus committed bank financing from JPMorgan, Citigroup and MUFG, [as detailed in the announcement](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260426881370/en/Sun-Pharma-signs-Definitive-Agreement-to-Acquire-Organon).

Closing requires Organon stockholder approval and customary antitrust and regulatory clearances, with the companies targeting completion in early 2027, [as detailed in the announcement](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260426881370/en/Sun-Pharma-signs-Definitive-Agreement-to-Acquire-Organon).

## What We Don't Know

The announcement does not specify the future of Organon as a brand or how its New Jersey corporate functions will be integrated with Sun Pharma's Mumbai headquarters. The companies have not disclosed termination fees, go-shop provisions or detailed timelines for individual regulatory filings. It is also unclear how Organon's substantial existing debt — most of it raised when Merck spun the company off in 2021 — will be refinanced or retained on the combined balance sheet beyond the announced bridge package.

Sun Pharma also has not detailed how it will handle Organon's manufacturing footprint, [described in the Organon press release](https://www.organon.com/news/sun-pharma-signs-definitive-agreement-to-acquire-organon/) as comprising six manufacturing facilities, or whether the integration will trigger workforce changes. The companies say a more detailed integration plan will follow regulatory clearance.

## Industry Context

The Organon transaction is the largest biopharma deal announced so far in 2026, [according to Fierce Pharma](https://www.fiercepharma.com/pharma/sun-pharma-strikes-biopharmas-largest-deal-26-1175b-buyout-organon). It also signals that Indian pharmaceutical companies, long associated with low-margin generic exports, are now willing to underwrite scale-defining transactions in developed markets — a shift [Bloomberg highlighted](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-27/sun-pharma-to-acquire-us-listed-organon-in-12-billion-deal) by noting this is the largest overseas acquisition ever made by an Indian drugmaker.
